matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules also receive the actual sample REF -> ALT change; curated allele-change rules take precedence when the observed ALT allele or exact change has a known direction.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a cell-cycle checkpoint and tumor-suppressor thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of p16INK4A/p14ARF, RB-p53, senescence, melanoma, pancreatic cancer, glioma, or broader cancer-risk biology because CDKN2A controls G1 arrest and oncogenic-stress response. Only known loss-of-function or pathogenic CDKN2A variants should be treated as high-risk clinical findings.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: Observed rs11515 suggests this sample carries the CDKN2A prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a cell-cycle checkpoint and tumor-suppressor thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of p16INK4A/p14ARF, RB-p53, senescence, melanoma, pancreatic cancer, glioma, or broader cancer-risk biology because CDKN2A controls G1 arrest and oncogenic-stress response. Only known loss-of-function or pathogenic CDKN2A variants should be treated as high-risk clinical findings. This specific marker is curated as CDKN2A 3'UTR regulatory SNP. rs11515 is a 3'UTR CDKN2A polymorphism curated here as a research-level regulatory marker. It has been discussed in melanoma and cancer-risk literature but is not treated as a pathogenic stand-alone allele in this local database.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs11515 with the actual DNA change {change}. Anchor the CDKN2A prediction to that observed REF -> ALT result instead of treating the variant as only a theoretical marker. The current preview does not infer zygosity or allele dose, so dosage-specific conclusions require genotype fields.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Likely reflects post-transcriptional regulatory context at the CDKN2A locus.

evidence.label: PubMed 26557774: CDKN2A 3'UTR sequence variants in melanoma

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a cell-cycle checkpoint and tumor-suppressor thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of p16INK4A/p14ARF, RB-p53, senescence, melanoma, pancreatic cancer, glioma, or broader cancer-risk biology because CDKN2A controls G1 arrest and oncogenic-stress response. Only known loss-of-function or pathogenic CDKN2A variants should be treated as high-risk clinical findings. An observed CDKN2A variant places this sample in the curated CDKN2A research context. The bundled CDKN2A knowledge base is tumor-suppressor focused. Clinically important CDKN2A events are often rare germline variants, deletions, or promoter methylation changes rather than common low-effect polymorphisms. For CDKN2A, rare germline and somatic loss-of-function events usually carry more weight than common SNPs.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: CDKN2A is a major tumor-suppressor locus in melanoma, pancreatic cancer predisposition, glioma, and many additional tumors.
The locus is also central to aging and senescence research because p16INK4A expression is a canonical cellular-senescence marker.
Cancer-associated promoter methylation studies.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a CDKN2A variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a cell-cycle checkpoint and tumor-suppressor thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of p16INK4A/p14ARF, RB-p53, senescence, melanoma, pancreatic cancer, glioma, or broader cancer-risk biology because CDKN2A controls G1 arrest and oncogenic-stress response. Only known loss-of-function or pathogenic CDKN2A variants should be treated as high-risk clinical findings. CDKN2A methylation is often biologically meaningful because promoter hypermethylation is a recurrent mode of tumor-suppressor silencing. In this workbench it should still be read as regulatory context rather than as a stand-alone diagnosis. The bundled CDKN2A knowledge base is tumor-suppressor focused. Clinically important CDKN2A events are often rare germline variants, deletions, or promoter methylation changes rather than common low-effect polymorphisms. For CDKN2A, rare germline and somatic loss-of-function events usually carry more weight than common SNPs.
